ogTitle: Brookton vs Broomehill East: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Brookton and Broomehill East.
heading: Brookton vs Broomehill East — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Brookton, Western Australia and Broomehill East, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Brookton has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Broomehill East (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Brookton or Broomehill East safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Brookton has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Broomehill East scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Brookton scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Broomehill East scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Brookton or Broomehill East?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Brookton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
